content.claim: Early tracking is associated with increased inequality of opportunity across OECD countries, with a social achievement gap widening by approximately 6.90 score points (effect size d=0.44)
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Meta-analysis aggregates diverse country contexts with differing tracking mechanisms
Effect size measured via specific assessment tools (books in home proxy for SES); may not generalize to all inequality dimensions
Causal interpretation assumes tracking age is exogenous; policy adoption may correlate with pre-existing inequality
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
